entries.response:
Here is a sample CodeFlow Diagram generated by using PlantUML with @startuml:
```
+---------------+
|                                         |
|    Y|                               |
|  |                                         |
|  |               |  |                                         |
|  |               |  |                                         |
|  |               |  |                                         |
|  |               |  |                                         |
+---------------+
|  |                |  |                             |  |  |   |
|  |                |  |                             |  |  |   |  |
|  |                |  |                |  |  |   |  |  |
|  |                |  |                |  |  |   |  |  |
+---------------+
```
In this diagram, the `Y` code is shown in green, with `"Hello World"` at the top of the diagram and a `/` symbol in the middle. The `@startuml` tag links to the actual code inside the `Y`.

Here is an example of a CodeFlow Diagram generated by using PlantUML with @startuml:
```
+---------------+
...
                    
    Hello World
     /   \  \
     /     \  \      \
     /    \  \      \

    Error: Missing @startuml or @enduml tags
     ...
    Please correct the code and return ONLY valid PlantUML code wrapped in @startuml and @enduml tags.
```
